observable_failures: ACP does not accurately reflect email validation status of users (validated, pending, expired, or missing)
Expired confirmation keys prevent email validation even when they should still be actionable
Email status display in ACP is unclear or incorrect
"Validate" action fails when expected data (confirm:<code> object) is missing
"Send validation email" action fails when expected email data is missing or keys have expired
No fallback mechanism to locate user emails when primary key lookup fails
repair_targets: Accurate display of email status in ACP (validated, pending, expired, or missing) via email:pending and email:expired flags on user objects
Email confirmation remains valid until explicitly expired (use stored expires timestamp, not DB-level TTL)
Validation actions fallback to alternative sources (confirm:<code> object email field) to locate user emails when primary user profile email is missing
User.validateEmail handler retrieves email via getEmailForValidation and saves it to user profile before confirming
User.sendValidationEmail handler obtains correct email via getEmailForValidation and passes it explicitly
User account deletion invokes User.email.expireValidation(uid) to clean up pending confirmation data
New getConfirmObjs() helper fetches confirmation codes using db.mget() and resolves expiry timestamps
regression_expectations: Existing database adapter API contracts must be respected (no signature changes to existing methods)
Standard or high-quality image/user inputs must not be negatively impacted by changes
loadUserInfo() must still return all existing user fields; only new flags are added
Existing confirmation expiry logic (database TTL) must not be broken for other code paths that rely on it

semantic_boundaries: db.mget(keys: string[]): Promise<(string | null)[]> — batch retrieve values by keys, preserving input order; null for missing keys
user.email.getEmailForValidation(uid: number): Promise<string | null> — falls back from user:<uid> email to confirm:<code> email, only if UID matches
User.email.isValidationPending(uid, email): returns true only if confirmation object exists, current time < expires timestamp, and email matches if provided
User.email.canSendValidation(uid, email): interval check uses stored TTL timestamp (or current time as baseline) + configured interval against max confirmation period
behavioral_constraints: TO-BE: db.mget must be implemented in src/database/mongo/main.js, src/database/postgres/main.js, and src/database/redis/main.js
TO-BE: db.mget must use client.mget for Redis, $in filter on _key for MongoDB, JOIN of legacy_object_live and legacy_string for PostgreSQL
TO-BE: db.mget must preserve input key order and return null at the corresponding index for any key not found
TO-BE: loadUserInfo(callerUid, uids) must attach email:pending and email:expired flags derived by resolving confirm:byUid:<uid> keys via getConfirmObjs()
TO-BE: getConfirmObjs() within loadUserInfo() uses db.mget() on confirm:byUid:<uid> keys then db.getObjects() on confirm:<code> keys
TO-BE: User.email.sendValidationEmail must store expires as Unix timestamp in milliseconds in confirm:<code> instead of relying on DB-level TTL
TO-BE: user.email.getEmailForValidation must first try user:<uid> email, then fallback to confirm:<code> email field
TO-BE: user.email.expireValidation(uid) must be called on user account deletion
backward_compatibility: Existing db.get, db.getObject, db.set, db.sortedSetAdd signatures must not be changed
loadUserInfo return shape must remain backward compatible (existing fields unchanged, new flags added)
Database adapter method signatures outside of mget must not change
similar_implementation_patterns: Existing db.getObject() and db.getObjects() patterns in database adapters as reference for mget implementation
Existing user.email.confirmByUid() pattern for how confirmation logic currently works
Existing TTL/expires handling in confirmation system as baseline for new timestamp-based expiry
missing_elements_to_implement: db.mget(keys: string[]): Promise<(string | null)[]> in src/database/mongo/main.js
db.mget(keys: string[]): Promise<(string | null)[]> in src/database/postgres/main.js
db.mget(keys: string[]): Promise<(string | null)[]> in src/database/redis/main.js
getConfirmObjs() helper function within loadUserInfo() in src/user/index.js or related user module
user.email.getEmailForValidation(uid: number): Promise<string | null> in src/user/email.js
User.email.expireValidation(uid) invocation on user account deletion (caller site and method must both exist)

suspect_entities: src/user/index.js or src/user/*.js — loadUserInfo function missing email:pending and email:expired flag logic
src/user/email.js — missing getEmailForValidation, insufficient isValidationPending/canSendValidation, missing expires timestamp storage in sendValidationEmail
src/user/email.js — User.validateEmail handler missing getEmailForValidation call
src/user/email.js — User.sendValidationEmail handler missing getEmailForValidation call and email passthrough
User deletion path — missing User.email.expireValidation(uid) call
src/database/mongo/main.js — db.mget EXISTS but reads only d.data, never d.value (latent inconsistency)
src/database/postgres/main.js — db.mget EXISTS but has LIMIT 1 bug causing incorrect results with multiple keys
src/database/redis/main.js — db.mget EXISTS and appears correct
src/controllers/admin/users.js:170-207 — loadUserInfo correctly uses db.mget + db.getObjects to derive email:pending and email:expired flags

src/user/email.js — all functions exist: getEmailForValidation (54-67), isValidationPending (69-75), canSendValidation (92-103), sendValidationEmail with expires timestamp (166), expireValidation (83-90)
src/socket.io/admin/user.js — User.validateEmail (65-77) and User.sendValidationEmail (79-104) correctly call getEmailForValidation
src/user/email.js:128-130 — sendValidationEmail falls back to stored email (not confirm object email) when options.email is not provided — potential wrong email sent for pending email changes
call_chain_context: getUsers (controllers/admin/users.js) -> loadUserInfo -> db.mget(confirm:byUid:<uid> keys) -> db.getObjects(confirm:<code> keys) -> email:pending/email:expired flags
UserEmail.confirmByCode (email.js:186) -> reads confirm:<code> -> calls expireValidation on expiry/mismatch
dataflow_relevant_uses: confirm:byUid:<uid> key stores the active code string; read by getEmailForValidation, isValidationPending, canSendValidation, expireValidation, loadUserInfo
confirm:<code> hash stores {email, uid, expires}; expires = Date.now() + emailConfirmExpiry hours
confirmObj.expires used by isValidationPending (email.js:72) and loadUserInfo (admin/users.js:200-201) to determine expired vs pending
user.email.getEmailForValidation reads confirm:byUid:<uid> then confirm:<code>.email when uid matches
User.email.sendValidationEmail stores expires as Date.now() + emailConfirmExpiry * 3600000 (milliseconds) at email.js:166

must_co_edit_relations: src/database/mongo/main.js, src/database/postgres/main.js, src/database/redis/main.js — db.mget must be implemented in all three adapters simultaneously
src/user/email.js — getEmailForValidation must be called by both User.validateEmail and User.sendValidationEmail
src/user/index.js (or user/info.js) — loadUserInfo must be updated to use getConfirmObjs() and attach email:pending and email:expired flags
User deletion path — wherever user deletion is handled must call User.email.expireValidation(uid)
src/database/postgres/main.js — LIMIT 1 bug in mget SQL must be fixed for bulk confirmation lookups to work correctly
src/controllers/admin/users.js:174-179 — depends on db.mget returning results in input key order; LIMIT 1 bug causes incorrect results
src/user/email.js:128-130 — sendValidationEmail fallback uses stored email instead of pending confirm email; may send to wrong address for pending email changes
dependency_propagation: db.mget implementation in all three adapters is a prerequisite for getConfirmObjs() in loadUserInfo()
getConfirmObjs() result (confirm objects with expires timestamps) feeds the email:pending and email:expired flags in loadUserInfo()
User.email.sendValidationEmail storing expires timestamp in the confirmation object enables isValidationPending() and canSendValidation() correctness
getEmailForValidation() result is consumed by User.validateEmail (to set user field) and User.sendValidationEmail (to pass as email option)
User.email.expireValidation(uid) cleanup prevents stale confirmation data from persisting after user deletion
db.mget in postgres has LIMIT 1 bug — when multiple keys passed, only first result returned, breaking loadUserInfo email:pending/email:expired flag computation
db.mget correctness in all three adapters is prerequisite for loadUserInfo to display accurate email validation status in ACP
email.js:128-130 fallback logic determines what email receives validation message when ACP resends for pending email changes
